seo.title: Google Ads Target ROAS (tROAS): How to Set It from Margin
seo.description: Set Google Ads target ROAS from break-even economics — not guesswork. Learn where to find margin data, how tROAS bidding works, and common mistakes.

author.name: Ad Breakeven Editorial
author.bio: Practical break-even math for ecommerce and lead gen. We built Ad Breakeven because generic ROAS benchmarks ignore margin — our articles use contribution economics, not platform vanity metrics.

headline: How to Set Google Ads Target ROAS from Your Margin
intro: Target ROAS (tROAS) tells Google Smart Bidding the efficiency floor you need. Set it from break-even economics — margin, AOV, and fixed costs — not from a competitor benchmark.

sections.heading: What target ROAS does in Google Ads

blocks.text: Target ROAS bidding optimizes for conversion value relative to spend. Google uses your target as a signal — campaigns may fluctuate above or below it as the algorithm learns. Your break-even ROAS is the minimum target that keeps campaigns profitable after costs Google does not see.

sections.heading: Calculate break-even ROAS before opening Google Ads

items: Find gross margin from Shopify, your P&L, or COGS spreadsheet
Subtract per-order fixed costs (shipping, payment fees, packaging)
Divide AOV by contribution per order
Add profit headroom if you want margin above break-even
Using revenue ROAS from Google as if it included COGS
Copying a competitor's 400% target without matching margin
Setting tROAS below break-even to get more volume
Ignoring conversion value rules — ensure value reflects AOV accurately

faq.question: What target ROAS should I use in Google Ads?
faq.answer: Start at or above your break-even ROAS calculated from margin and AOV. Add headroom for profit goals. Do not use generic 400% unless your economics support it.

faq.question: Where do I find margin for tROAS?
faq.answer: Shopify gross profit reports, accounting P&L, or COGS divided by revenue. Use true gross margin before ad spend, not net profit.

faq.question: Can tROAS be lower than break-even ROAS?
faq.answer: You can set it lower, but the campaign will lose money after product costs. Lower tROAS may increase volume at the expense of profitability.
